name: Current Affairs in Indian Economy & TN Dev. Admin. (10): MSP 2026-27 & PM-KISAN Allocation

sourceRefs.label: ddnews.gov.in/zeebiz.com - CCEA's MSP approval for 14 Kharif crops (2026-27); estimated ₹2.60 lakh crore farmer payout; PM-KISAN's ₹63,500cr Budget 2026-27 allocation

researchNotes: Thirteenth Economy current-affairs topic (10th lap). MSP figures are dated to a specific marketing season (2026-27) and approved by a specific Cabinet body (CCEA) — a stable, citable policy decision rather than a constantly-revising statistic.

reading.intro: Covers the Cabinet's MSP approval for Kharif crops (2026-27) and the PM-KISAN scheme's Budget 2026-27 allocation.

qa.question: Which Cabinet body approved the Minimum Support Price (MSP) increase for 14 Kharif crops for the 2026-27 marketing season, and who chairs it?
qa.answer: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA), chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

qa.question: Which crop received the highest absolute MSP increase for 2026-27, and by how much?
qa.answer: Sunflower seed — a hike of ₹622 per quintal.

qa.question: Name the next 3 crops (after sunflower seed) with the largest MSP increases, and their amounts.
What is the estimated total farmer payout from MSP operations for the 2026-27 Kharif season?
qa.answer: Approximately ₹2.60 lakh crore — one of the largest agricultural procurement support mechanisms.

qa.question: What was PM-KISAN's allocation in the Union Budget 2026-27?

questions.prompt: The MSP increase for 14 Kharif crops (2026-27 season) was approved by:
options: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA)
The Agricultural Costs and Prices Commission alone
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA).
